testcase.name: test_request_success
testcase.failure:
mock_requests_get = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4565255952'> fivesim = <ninjemail.sms_services.fivesim.FiveSim object at 0x110216510>
def test_request_success(mock_requests_get, fivesim): > response = fivesim.request("some_command") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
ninjemail/tests/test_sms_5sim.py:28: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ninjemail/sms_services/fivesim.py:89: in request return json.loads(response_data) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/json/__init__.py:346: in loads return _default_decoder.decode(s) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/json/decoder.py:337: in decode obj, end = self.raw_decode(s, idx=_w(s, 0).end()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <json.decoder.JSONDecoder object at 0x1014fe3d0>, s = '', idx = 0
def raw_decode(self, s, idx=0): """Decode a JSON document from ``s`` (a ``str`` beginning with a JSON document) and return a 2-tuple of the Python representation and the index in ``s`` where the document ended.
This can be used to decode a JSON document from a string that may have extraneous data at the end.
""" try: obj, end = self.scan_once(s, idx) except StopIteration as err: > raise JSONDecodeError("Expecting value", s, err.value) from None E json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/json/decoder.py:355: JSONDecodeError

testcase.name: test_request_error_no_free_phones
testcase.failure:
mock_requests_get = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4563516176'> fivesim = <ninjemail.sms_services.fivesim.FiveSim object at 0x110271b90>
def test_request_error_no_free_phones(mock_requests_get, fivesim): mock_requests_get.return_value.response_buffer.write(b'no free phones') mock_requests_get.return_value.perform.side_effect = None
with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info: > fivesim.request('no_phone')

testcase.name: test_request_error_not_enough_balance
testcase.failure:
mock_requests_get = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4418263120'> fivesim = <ninjemail.sms_services.fivesim.FiveSim object at 0x1075f8b50>
def test_request_error_not_enough_balance(mock_requests_get, fivesim): mock_requests_get.return_value.response_buffer.write(b'not enough user balance') mock_requests_get.return_value.perform.side_effect = None
with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info: > fivesim.request('no_balance')

testcase.name: test_get_code_error_response

failure.message:
AssertionError: assert 'Expecting va...mn 1 (char 0)' == 'Test exception'
- Test exception + Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)

testcase.failure:
mock_requests_get = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4418635856'> mock_time_sleep = <MagicMock name='sleep' id='4425404432'> fivesim = <ninjemail.sms_services.fivesim.FiveSim object at 0x107c657d0>
def test_get_code_error_response(mock_requests_get, mock_time_sleep, fivesim): mock_time_sleep.side_effect = Exception("Test exception") mock_requests_get.return_value.response_buffer.write(b'{"status": "CANCELLED", "sms": []}') with pytest.raises(Exception) as exc_info: fivesim.get_code("order123") > assert str(exc_info.value) == "Test exception" E AssertionError: assert 'Expecting va...mn 1 (char 0)' == 'Test exception' E E - Test exception E + Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)
ninjemail/tests/test_sms_5sim.py:90: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_request
testcase.failure:
mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4426121488'> gs = <ninjemail.sms_services.getsmscode.GetsmsCode object at 0x107d17390>
@patch("pycurl.Curl") def test_request(mock_curl, gs): mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = "Success" > response = gs.request(action="getmobile") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
ninjemail/tests/test_sms_getsmscode.py:48: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ninjemail.sms_services.getsmscode.GetsmsCode object at 0x107d17390> kwargs = {'action': 'getmobile'}, buffer = <_io.BytesIO object at 0x10756fdd0> c = <MagicMock name='Curl()' id='4426135440'> post_fields = {'action': 'getmobile', 'pid': '123', 'token': 'token', 'username': 'user'} http_code = 'Success'
def request(self, **kwargs): """ Sends a POST request to the GetsmsCode API with the provided arguments.
Args: kwargs (dict): Additional arguments to be included in the request body.
Returns: str: The API response text.
Raises: APIError: If the API returns an error message. """ if self.country not in {"us", "cn"}: kwargs["cocode"] = self.country
buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, self.API_URL) c.setopt(c.POST, 1) c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) post_fields = { 'username': self.user, 'token': self.token, 'pid': self.project, **kwargs, } c.setopt(c.POSTFIELDS, '&'.join([f"{key}={value}" for key, value in post_fields.items()]))
try: c.perform() http_code = c.getinfo(c.RESPONSE_CODE) if http_code != 200: > raise APIError(f"HTTP error: {http_code}") E ninjemail.sms_services.getsmscode.APIError: HTTP error: Success
ninjemail/sms_services/getsmscode.py:119: APIError

testcase.name: test_get_phone
testcase.failure:
mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4425211216'> gs = <ninjemail.sms_services.getsmscode.GetsmsCode object at 0x107553250>
@patch("pycurl.Curl") def test_get_phone(mock_curl, gs): mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = "234567" > phone = gs.get_phone() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
ninjemail/tests/test_sms_getsmscode.py:55: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ninjemail/sms_services/getsmscode.py:148: in get_phone data = self.request(**data) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ninjemail.sms_services.getsmscode.GetsmsCode object at 0x107553250> kwargs = {'action': 'getmobile'}, buffer = <_io.BytesIO object at 0x107e19760> c = <MagicMock name='Curl()' id='4425205584'> post_fields = {'action': 'getmobile', 'pid': '123', 'token': 'token', 'username': 'user'} http_code = '234567'
def request(self, **kwargs): """ Sends a POST request to the GetsmsCode API with the provided arguments.
Args: kwargs (dict): Additional arguments to be included in the request body.
Returns: str: The API response text.
Raises: APIError: If the API returns an error message. """ if self.country not in {"us", "cn"}: kwargs["cocode"] = self.country
buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, self.API_URL) c.setopt(c.POST, 1) c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) post_fields = { 'username': self.user, 'token': self.token, 'pid': self.project, **kwargs, } c.setopt(c.POSTFIELDS, '&'.join([f"{key}={value}" for key, value in post_fields.items()]))
try: c.perform() http_code = c.getinfo(c.RESPONSE_CODE) if http_code != 200: > raise APIError(f"HTTP error: {http_code}") E ninjemail.sms_services.getsmscode.APIError: HTTP error: 234567
ninjemail/sms_services/getsmscode.py:119: APIError

testcase.name: test_get_phone_with_prefix
testcase.failure:
mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4426822928'> gs = <ninjemail.sms_services.getsmscode.GetsmsCode object at 0x107dbcdd0>
@patch("pycurl.Curl") def test_get_phone_with_prefix(mock_curl, gs): mock_curl.return_value.perform.return_value = None mock_curl.return_value.getinfo.return_value = "1234567" > phone = gs.get_phone(send_prefix=True) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
ninjemail/tests/test_sms_getsmscode.py:62: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ninjemail/sms_services/getsmscode.py:148: in get_phone data = self.request(**data) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ninjemail.sms_services.getsmscode.GetsmsCode object at 0x107dbcdd0> kwargs = {'action': 'getmobile'}, buffer = <_io.BytesIO object at 0x107d13e20> c = <MagicMock name='Curl()' id='4426816336'> post_fields = {'action': 'getmobile', 'pid': '123', 'token': 'token', 'username': 'user'} http_code = '1234567'
def request(self, **kwargs): """ Sends a POST request to the GetsmsCode API with the provided arguments.
Args: kwargs (dict): Additional arguments to be included in the request body.
Returns: str: The API response text.
Raises: APIError: If the API returns an error message. """ if self.country not in {"us", "cn"}: kwargs["cocode"] = self.country
buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, self.API_URL) c.setopt(c.POST, 1) c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) post_fields = { 'username': self.user, 'token': self.token, 'pid': self.project, **kwargs, } c.setopt(c.POSTFIELDS, '&'.join([f"{key}={value}" for key, value in post_fields.items()]))
try: c.perform() http_code = c.getinfo(c.RESPONSE_CODE) if http_code != 200: > raise APIError(f"HTTP error: {http_code}") E ninjemail.sms_services.getsmscode.APIError: HTTP error: 1234567
ninjemail/sms_services/getsmscode.py:119: APIError
